title: News in Frames: Fastest hooves first on the track
content: Widely regarded as one of Maharashtra’s most prestigious bullock cart races, the Hindkesari Bailgada Sharyat once again drew a massive turnout in Pedgaon

title: Pandavani legend Teejan Bai dies at 70
content: Teejan Bai was widely regarded as the foremost exponent of Pandavani, the traditional folk art form of Chhattisgarh that narrates episodes from the Indian epic Mahabharata

title: Operative behind recce of Ram temple and RSS HQ, Hafiz Saeed's relative in designated terrorist list
content: All 23 of them were designated as terrorist under fourth schedule of the Unlawful Activities (Prevention) Act (UAPA)

title: Search for two LeT terrorists enters second day in J&K's Shopian
content: The searches have been resumed to track down the two terrorists who were spotted by surveillance cameras on Friday (July 3, 2026) in an orchard in the Meemandar area, which comprises seven villages

title: Eknath Shinde admitted to hospital, condition stable
content: Doctors attributed the ailment to a combination of viral fever and physical exhaustion from sustained activity

title: BJP will register a decisive victory in U.P., says party chief Nitin Nabin
content: Mr. Nabin, who reached Lucknow on a two-day visit, added that he was overwhelmed by the respect received in the land of Lord Shri Ram.

title: EAM Jaishankar to embark on 6-nation tour from July 5
content: External Affairs Minister S. Jaishankar's visit to the Gulf nations comes against the backdrop of fast-evolving political dynamics in the region following the peace agreement between the U.S. and  Iran

title: Finance Ministry notifies rules for determination of origin of goods under India-U.K. trade pact
content: CETA secures duty-free access for 99% of India’s exports to the U.K., covering nearly the entire trade basket

title: Delhi court rejects Umar Khalid, Sharjeel Imam bail pleas, says bound by Supreme Court order
content: Trial court holds that it has ‘no option’ but to follow the Supreme Court’s January order; defence relies on the top court’s subsequent ruling in Syed Iftikhar Andrabi v. NIA which it said reaffirmed the principle that prolonged incarceration could justify bail

title: Hyderabad Police seize 122 tonnes of adulterated food, book 185 cases in H-FAST’s first 100 days
content: Rajendranagar recorded the highest number of cases at 55, followed by Golconda (44), Secunderabad (35), Charminar (21), Shamshabad (13), Jubilee Hills (12) and Khairatabad (5)

title: India weathered West Asia crisis through strategic decision-making, diplomacy: PM Modi
content: India avoided fuel rationing, LPG disruptions, price surges, with domestic refineries upping their LPG production and public sector OMCs bearing the losses, says PM; promises further expansion of refinery capacity

title: Railway ministry postpones formal launch of bullet train tunneling work amid Mumbai rains
content: Ministry officials said it has been raining in Mumbai for the past few days, and IMD has issued 'red' and 'orange' alerts for the next couple of days

title: Bihar to set up 100 fast track courts
content: The Chief Minister asserted that the state’s judiciary, police, and administration bear the major responsibility in delivering justice to a population of over 14 crores representing approximately 10% of the country’s total population.

title: Roadside vendors turned exporters: Meghalaya’s ‘numbered’ farmers reap fruits of labour
content: A 433-member organic farmers’ producer company in Ri-Bhoi district’s Jirang has become a model of successful farm collectivisation

title: Experts emphasise E20 compliant, thoroughly tested amid raging concerns
content: It said as per its service protocol, the fuel tank and fuel lines were drained, thoroughly cleaned and the vehicle was refilled with Standard E20 fuel.

title: Fresh political support pours in on 15th day of CJP protest; student hospitalised
content: Activist Sonam Wangchuk continues seventh day of his hunger strike; political leaders say will raise the issue of irregularities in examinations during Parliament’s upcoming Monsoon Session

title: NDPS cases, arrests double in Kochi in a month
content: Of the 1,143 drug-related cases registered in the first six months of the year, 503 were recorded in June alone following the launch of the new anti-drug operation

title: Ninth edition of The Hindu Home Expo commences at LuLu Mall
content: Mayor hails the expo as ‘a bridge to Keralite’s housing dreams’. Among the key highlights of the event are special rate offers from developers, reduced bank interest rates, and on-the-spot interactions with property developers

title: Traffic snarls worsen around Kochi metro extension, NHAI flyover worksites
content: Police say unscientific site management, potholes and inadequate supervision at the ongoing works have worsened traffic snarls on key arterial roads

title: J&K L-G administration suspends 8 officials over pro-separatist content in library books
content: An official spokesman said the book, “Personalities and Legends of J&K”, contained content related to separatism, which has the potential to create law and order situations

title: Two DMK MLAs were offered Ministry post, money to join TVK, alleges R.S. Bharathi
content: A delegation from the DMK met Governor Rajendra Viswanath Arlekar and submitted a memorandum seeking action into allegations of ‘horse-trading’ by the ruling party and over the alleged participation of two private individuals in a Cabinet meeting

title: Prepare contingency plan for sowing amid delayed monsoon: Official
content: Officials told to ensure adequate stocks of seeds and fertilizers and prepare for drinking water scarcity if dry spell continues.

title: Install aerators, use drinking water judiciously, BWSSB tells citizens
content: Given a severely deficit monsoon this year, with water levels in reservoirs already running low, the summer of 2027 is expected to be parched

title: AIADMK complains to Arlekar over ‘inaction’ on its petitions on ‘horse trading’
content: Speaking to reports after meeting Arlekar, the party’s agricultural wing secretary and whip in the Assembly ‘Agri’ S.S. Krishnamurthy said there was no follow up to his party’s memorandum given to Assembly Speaker J.C.D. Prabhakar

title: It’s time for Tamil Nadu to segregate solid waste at source
content: Though the new Solid Waste Management Rules kicked in on April 1, 2026, there has been little awareness among residents of this momentous change in urban solid waste management. While most parts of the world have settled into a routine of segregation of waste at source, Tamil Nadu still packs a mixed garbage bag. This report looks at how the new Rules could impact source segregation in the State

title: 60% of ammonia gas cleared from seafood factory in Tiruvallur
content: The operation was undertaken by the district administration and a team of experts; work to remove the remaining gas will resume today

title: Monsoon tracker July 4 Highlights: IMD issues red alert in five districts in Odisha
content: Incessant and heavy rains threw life out of gear in Mumbai and its metropolitan region; the monsoon is active in Gujarat with “exceptional heavy rainfall” in Junagadh, where a few areas witnessed severe waterlogging

title: Maharashtra TET scam: Three more persons arrested
content: Ten people have been arrested in the case so far; they have been remanded to police custody till July 9

title: Why tanks still matter in modern warfare
content: Drones, missiles and precision-guided weapons have transformed the modern battlefield, but military experts argue they cannot achieve the ultimate objective of war – controlling territory. Saurabh Trivedi examines why tanks and ground forces remain indispensable despite the rapid evolution of warfare, drawing on lessons from Ukraine and insights from senior Army officers

title: Houses, SUVs, Rs 5-6L stolen daily: How lifestyles of Ram temple theft accused changed dramatically
content: An investigation into Ram Temple donations in Ayodhya has revealed a stark financial uplift among accused individuals, with some building homes and acquiring SUVs despite modest known incomes. Evidence suggests significant daily siphoning of funds, estimated at Rs 6 to Rs 8 lakh, particularly during the Maha Kumbh period.  Concerns were raised about the handling of precious metal donations and the lack of proper oversight.

title: 'Unwell since his death': Ketan Agarwal's grandfather dies of cardiac arrest
content: The grandfather of Ketan Agarwal, a Pune realtor whose death at Lohagad Fort is being investigated as murder, has passed away weeks after his grandson's demise. Devichand Agarwal reportedly suffered a cardiac arrest following the shock. Police are investigating the alleged murder of Ketan Agarwal, with his fiancée, Siya Goyal, and her alleged lover arrested. A Snapchat message from Goyal, expressing doubts about the wedding, has surfaced.

title: France vs Paraguay becomes one of hottest World Cup matches ever
content: France lead Paraguay in a FIFA World Cup 2026 Round of 16 clash played in extreme heat at Philadelphia’s Lincoln Financial Field, with temperatures near 100°F (37.8°C) and heat index reaching 105–110°F (40.6–43.3°C). The extreme conditions, under an official heat warning, have turned the match into a severe physical test, with Kylian Mbappé’s penalty separating the sides.

title: Pandavani legend Teejan Bai dies at 70 after prolonged illness
content: Renowned Pandavani folk singer Teejan Bai, a Padma Vibhushan awardee, passed away at 70 in Raipur after a prolonged illness. Hailing from Chhattisgarh, she was celebrated for her powerful renditions of Mahabharata tales, elevating the traditional art form to global recognition. Her impactful performances and dedication to folk arts earned her India's highest civilian honours. She had been receiving treatment at AIIMS Raipur since late May.

title: Tim Cook, Sundar Pichai, Michael Dell and other tech CEOs share July 4 greetings
content: Tech titans like Tim Cook, Sundar Pichai, and Elon Musk joined the nation in celebrating America's 250th Independence Day. Leaders from Apple, Google, SpaceX, and others shared heartfelt messages on social media, reflecting on the country's journey and future. Michael Dell also announced a significant donation to support American children's financial futures, highlighting a blend of patriotic sentiment and forward-looking initiatives within the tech community.

title: Watch: New York's Brooklyn Bridge catches fire during July 4 fireworks display
content: During Macy's Fourth of July fireworks extravaganza, the stunning Brooklyn Bridge briefly caught fire, sending flames and dark smoke billowing up as fireworks lit up the night sky above. Eyewitnesses reported that the fire began modestly but intensified quickly, with additional explosions reportedly triggered by the blaze. The dramatic scene unfolded over approximately one minute, leaving uncertainty about any potential damage to the historic structure.

title: B'luru daycare horror: Whistleblower held; panel seeks live streaming at centres
content: Following alleged toddler abuse at a Bengaluru daycare, the Karnataka Commission for Protection of Child Rights will mandate CCTV cameras with live parental access at all centers. This move aims to enhance safety and transparency. The investigation into the incident, which involved a nanny allegedly locking a child in a bathroom and another nanny accused of suspicious video recording, is ongoing.

title: Sergey Brin sold his NYC real estate: Here's what Mamdani's rent freeze cost him
content: Google co-founder Sergey Brin has divested his stake in a New York City real estate fund for a fraction of its value, selling for just six cents on the dollar. This significant loss comes as Mayor Zohran Mamdani's rent freeze tightens conditions for landlords. The fund, A&E Real Estate, confirmed the sale, citing the challenging market for rent-stabilized apartments.

title: After 8,000 layoffs, Zuckerberg admits Meta misjudged AI progress
content: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg acknowledged internal restructuring hasn't yielded expected results, particularly in AI agent development, admitting executives 'miscalculated' timelines.  Despite significant layoffs and AI investments, progress has been slower than anticipated.  However, Zuckerberg anticipates substantial AI benefits within three to six months.  Separately, the company addressed mouse-tracking software, assuring no employee data was used for AI training and confirming future opt-in policies.

title: Gambhir's animated chat with Samson after Sooryavanshi's debut goes viral
content: Sanju Samson's omission from India's T20I against England, despite his World Cup heroics, has sparked debate. Head coach Gautam Gambhir, who previously lauded Samson as 'world-class,' was seen in a quiet conversation with the player before the match. Samson's recent low scores on the UK tour led to his replacement by debutant Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, highlighting the swift shifts in international cricket fortunes.

title: PoK 'infiltrator' sent back after crossing LoC to meet girlfriend in north Kashmir
content: A young man from Pakistan-occupied Kashmir, Zeeshan Mir, was repatriated home after crossing the Line of Control to meet his beloved, Iram Bano. Initially detained, Mir was eventually acquitted by an Uri court, which recognized his act as a desperate attempt of love rather than infiltration. The couple, whose families are related across the border, shared a brief meeting before Mir's handover, with both families hoping for their union.

title: 'He's not over her': Taylor Swift's ex boyfriend Matty Healy sparks fresh speculation
content: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce’s wedding has sparked chaos across the world and especially in New York City as billions wait for some wedding pictures to be leaked after they got married last night. Away from the chaos in New York City, Taylor Swift’s ex boyfriend, Matty Healy’s outing last evening has convinced many that he is not over her.

title: Serena Williams vs Venus Williams net worth comparison: Which sister is richer
content: Serena Williams and Venus Williams are more than just tennis legends. They are also smart businesswomen. Both sisters earned millions during their playing careers. But they did not stop there. They turned their success into businesses! They made smart investments, and got huge paydays through brand deals.

title: NHL rumors: Flames, Kraken eye Sabres' $3.75m rising scorer
content: The Buffalo Sabres are drawing trade interest in Jack Quinn as they seek to improve their roster after qualifying for the Stanley Cup Playoffs. The 24-year-old winger has a low cap hit and recent production, making him a strong trade candidate with the Calgary Flames and Seattle Kraken among the teams linked.

title: 'Ego holding Portugal hostage': Zlatan launches brutal attack on Ronaldo
content: Portugal's dramatic World Cup win over Croatia has sparked debate about Cristiano Ronaldo's starting role. Zlatan Ibrahimovic controversially stated that Portugal cannot win the 2026 World Cup with a 41-year-old Ronaldo leading the attack, suggesting his legendary status is overshadowing current form. Despite Ronaldo scoring, Gonçalo Ramos' late winner highlighted the team's potential, leaving many questioning the veteran's continued prominence.

title: Ukraine hits Russian oil terminal; smoke rises over St Petersburg facility - watch
content: Ukrainian drones targeted an oil terminal in St. Petersburg and military sites near the Baltic coast overnight, escalating attacks on Russia's energy sector. Governor Alexander Beglov confirmed the Kirovsky district oil terminal was hit, with no casualties.  President Zelensky claimed responsibility, stating the strikes hit revenue-generating oil infrastructure and the naval base at Kronstadt.  These actions come amid deepening fuel shortages across Russia, with long queues reported at petrol stations.

title: Trump offers to help end Ukraine war during 90-minute call with Putin, Zelenskyy
content: US President Trump has offered to help resolve the Ukraine conflict following separate calls with Russian President Putin and Ukrainian President Zelenskyy. While Russia claims battlefield advances, Ukraine disputes these assertions. Trump's envoys are set to continue mediation efforts ahead of the upcoming NATO summit, where the stalled peace talks and the ongoing war are expected to be key discussion points.

title: India bucking trend by adding refining capacity: PM Modi
content: Amidst global energy turmoil, India, under PM Modi's leadership, bolstered domestic LPG output and secured diverse crude supplies.  Strategic diplomacy and policy interventions successfully shielded citizens from potential shortages and price surges.  The inauguration of the HPCL Rajasthan Refinery marks a significant step in India's expanding refining prowess, positioning it as the world's fourth-largest refining nation.

title: Operation Entebbe: How Israel’s 1976 hostage rescue unfolded
content: In a remarkable display of tactical prowess, Israeli commandos executed Operation Thunderbolt under the cover of night at Entebbe Airport, successfully liberating over 100 hostages from terrorists. This high-stakes rescue unfolded after failed diplomatic negotiations, leading to a fierce debate over national sovereignty. The release of previously classified documents sheds light on the contentious discussions that paved the way for this audacious military intervention.

title: Xi says ready to work with Kim for 'long-term, sound and stable' China-North Korea ties
content: Chinese President Xi Jinping has expressed readiness to work with North Korean leader Kim Jong Un to foster long-term, stable bilateral ties. This comes amid escalating regional tensions and Pyongyang's deepening military collaboration with Russia. Xi's message, in response to Kim's congratulatory note, highlighted the historical bond and commitment to strengthening their strategic partnership, underscoring China's crucial economic role for North Korea.

title: MHA tags 17 from Pakistan and 6 Indians 'individual terrorists'
content: India has declared 23 individuals from Pakistan and Pakistan-occupied Kashmir as terrorists, targeting Lashkar-e-Taiba and Jaish operatives. Home Minister Amit Shah stated these individuals are involved in anti-India activities, including terror attacks and recruitment. The designated terrorists are linked to significant attacks like Pathankot and the Rameshwaram Cafe blast, with some also involved in reconnaissance for future strikes. This move underscores the government's firm stance against terrorism.

title: 'Alpha' box office day 2: Alia Bhatt's spy thriller crosses Rs 37 crore worldwide
content: Alia Bhatt's exhilarating spy thriller 'Alpha' has made a sensational debut, amassing ₹24 crore in India and ₹37 crore globally by its second day. Co-starring Sharvari, the film showcases impressive box office growth and captivated audiences right from the start. Kareena Kapoor Khan has praised the film's celebration of 'girl power', as it successfully engages viewers with its electrifying storyline.

title: Salah picks Messi over Ronaldo, Neymar and Kane for World Cup 'last dance'
content: Mohamed Salah has chosen Lionel Messi as his ideal “last dance” partner ahead of Egypt’s FIFA World Cup 2026 Round of 16 clash with Argentina in Atlanta. The showdown marks their first World Cup meeting, with Messi leading Argentina’s title defence and Salah chasing an upset. Both icons face a potentially defining knockout battle for a quarter-final place.

title: Carmakers on E20: No engine damage, but mileage drops
content: Vehicle manufacturers are defending the government's mandatory 20% ethanol blending in petrol, assuring the public that no engine damage has been observed. While acknowledging a slight drop in fuel efficiency for older vehicles due to lower calorific value, industry leaders state field experience has been positive. Discussions are ongoing with the government regarding flex-fuel vehicles as a future pathway.

title: 'Samne jayegi ki piche maaru?': Abhishek and Sooryavanshi's on-field chat goes viral
content: Fifteen-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, making his India debut, confidently advised senior partner Abhishek Sharma on pitch conditions during the second T20I against England.  Becoming India's youngest international debutant, Sooryavanshi impressed with a fearless 14 off 10 balls, including two sixes.  His historic entry and bold batting display captivated fans, showcasing remarkable composure on the international stage.

title: Govt sends notice to Telegram over pirated films, shows
content: Authorities have issued a stern notice to Telegram, demanding proactive measures against the rampant circulation of pirated films and shows. The government insists the messaging app must go beyond individual channel takedowns and implement robust systems to detect and remove infringing content.  This move follows previous concerns regarding Telegram's use for illegal activities, including fraud and the distribution of harmful material.  An action report is expected within fifteen days.

title: Monsoon session from July 20, govt hopes to push key bills in Parliament
content: The government aims to reintroduce its ambitious women's reservation bill in the upcoming monsoon session after a previous defeat. Speaker Om Birla's decision on defecting MPs could bolster the ruling alliance's numbers. Discussions are also expected on potential changes to the bill to address concerns from southern states, alongside a proposal for automatic removal of leaders facing serious criminal charges.

title: Inquiry report against Justice Varma will be tabled this session: Om Birla
content: Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla confirmed the report on corruption charges against Justice Yashwant Varma will be presented in the monsoon session, despite his resignation. The House will decide the next steps after the report's tabling, which could happen on the session's first day. Justice Varma faces allegations of financial irregularities following a fire incident at his Delhi residence last year.

title: BJP splitting parties, but won't get 2/3rd majority, says Congress
content: Congress leader Jairam Ramesh has accused the BJP of attempting to split opposition parties ahead of the monsoon session, warning that their true aim is to alter India's Constitution. Despite efforts, Ramesh asserted the ruling party will not achieve the two-thirds majority needed for such a move, citing the 'Ab ki Baar, 400 Paar' slogan's failure. He anticipates bills like delimitation and 'One Nation, One Election' might be introduced.

title: Desi defence platforms got Sindoor boost: Rajnath Singh
content: Defence Minister Rajnath Singh highlighted India's remarkable 12-year journey from shortages to self-reliance and confidence, culminating in the vision of a 'Viksit Bharat'. He emphasized the success of 'Make-in-India', particularly in defence, with production soaring and exports reaching record highs. Singh also underscored the media's vital role in national security and the nation's enhanced global standing, citing the abrogation of Article 370 and efforts against Naxalism as key achievements.

title: Monsoon to continue in 'active phase' over central India: IMD
content: Good news for monsoon-hit India! A low-pressure system over the Bay of Bengal is set to boost rainfall across central and western regions for the next five days, significantly reducing the deficit. While this brings relief for sowing, authorities are on alert for potential extreme weather events, with red alerts issued for several states. Monsoon is also advancing into northwest India, with full country coverage expected soon.

title: As vehicles get smarter, govt plans to thwart hijackings, roll out software update norms
content: India's road transport ministry is introducing mandatory cybersecurity and software update rules for advanced vehicles.  Starting October, new models with Level-3 automation and above will need to comply, followed by existing models and then vehicles with over-the-air update capabilities. This proactive measure addresses the growing threat of cyberattacks on increasingly connected cars, ensuring safer journeys for all.

title: 'Knows who the boss is': Trump says Netanyahu requested White House meeting next week
content: US President Donald Trump announced Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu requested a White House meeting, potentially next week. Trump highlighted their strong relationship, despite recent reports of differing views on regional issues like Iran and Lebanon. The visit, if confirmed, would be significant for Netanyahu ahead of Israel's upcoming elections. Trump also commented on Iran's eagerness for a deal and the funeral of former Iranian Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei.

title: 'Anti-national' school library book rocks J&K, 8 officials suspended
content: A political uproar has erupted in Jammu and Kashmir following the discovery of books in government school libraries describing the region as "India-occupied Kashmir" and referring to a banned separatist leader as "Shaheed." The BJP has demanded the education minister's resignation, while the administration has suspended officials, blacklisted publishers, and launched a high-level inquiry into the matter. This incident reignites the ongoing debate over narratives in the region.

title: 12k pilgrims visit Amarnath; 3rd batch of 4.8k leave for ice Shivling darshan
content: Thousands of pilgrims are continuing their journey to the sacred Shri Amarnath cave shrine. On Saturday, the third batch of 4,812 devotees departed from Jammu's Yatri Niwas base camp. This follows a successful opening day where over 12,000 pilgrims offered prayers. The administration has made advance registration compulsory, and helicopter services are unavailable due to the no-fly zone declaration along the pilgrimage route.

title: Hyderabad nurse caring for ailing mother stabbed to death by husband
content: A domestic dispute tragically escalated into murder in Chilkanagar, Hyderabad, where a 37-year-old nurse was fatally stabbed by her husband.  The accused, reportedly plagued by suspicions of infidelity, allegedly attacked his wife following an argument at their residence.  The victim succumbed to her injuries at the scene. Police have apprehended the suspect and are continuing their investigation into the incident.

title: AAI withdraws Srinagar airport closure for repairs, Omar Abdullah thanks govt
content: Srinagar airport's planned Monday-Tuesday closures and a full shutdown from October 1-16 have been withdrawn by AAI. The Indian Air Force had proposed these closures for runway repairs. Chief Minister Omar Abdullah expressed gratitude for the government's prompt response, highlighting the importance of uninterrupted air connectivity for tourism and the region's economy, especially during peak holiday seasons.

title: Bethell breaks Indian hearts on Sooryavanshi's debut; England take 1-0 lead
content: Bethell's unbeaten 76 off 46 balls helped England chase down 191 with an over to spare, giving the hosts a four-wicket win in the second match of the five-game series on Saturday.

title: Ex-CFO of RCap, Amit Bapna, held in connection with ADAG probe
content: The Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI) has apprehended Amit Bapna, former CFO of Reliance Capital Ltd, as part of its probe into Reliance ADA Group matters. Authorities allege Bapna, a key financial decision-maker, approved loans to intermediary firms despite knowing it violated RBI guidelines. Funds were reportedly diverted to various Reliance group entities, causing losses to banks and gains for the accused.

title: Modi greets Trump, Americans on 250th anniversary of Independence
content: The signing of the American Declaration of Independence, one of history's most-celebrated articulations of democratic ambitions, is being marked in myriad ways

title: Innovate and become job creators, Odisha Governor exhorts students
content: Update your knowledge in line with rapid technological advancements and explore opportunities in emerging fields such as AI, data science, robotics and quantum technology, advises Hari Babu at the Graduation Day ceremony at RVR & JC College of Engineering

title: As monsoon rains falter, Wayanad farmers in Kerala fear poor harvest
content: In Wayanad, rainfall deficit has adversely affected agriculture. The district received only 270.23 mm of rain in June, the lowest for the month in the past five years, according to Hume Centre for Ecology and Wildlife Biology

title: Mamata loyalist Chandrima Bhattacharya resigns as Bengal State president of Trinamool Congress
content: Her resignation is yet another setback for ex-CM Mamata Banerjee, who has only a handful of loyalists left in her camp; Ms. Bhattacharya also met Leader of Opposition Ritabrata Banerjee; she says once the ‘fundamental binding forces are gone, there is no reason for one to stay in the party or return to it’

title: Custodial death case: Sai Krishna’s mother pleads police for his mortal remains
content: Family members of the victim depose before the magisterial inquiry; mother alleges slow investigation

title: Assault case: T.N. Minister Marie Wilson told to appear before Puducherry court without fail on July 10
content: Mr. Wilson was supposed to appear before the court on Saturday to receive the additional charge sheet. However, his advocate informed the court of his inability to appear due to his Ministerial responsibilities

title: Minister inaugurates restored building of District Museum in Vellore fort complex
content: Prior to the restored building, the district museum was cramped with visitors due to limited space. Some artefacts were kept in the open, outside the building

title: Driven By Love, Divided By Border: PoK Man Who Crossed LoC For Lover Sent Back
content: The duo planned that Mir would cross the border, surrender directly to the army, and serve a legal sentence that was mandated for illegal entry into the country.

title: 'Thought People Hated Him': Trump On Iranians Crying At Khamenei's Funeral
content: Ali Khamenei, who ruled Iran for 36 years, was killed on February 28 - the day the US and Israel began striking Iran, triggering a war in the Middle East.

title: On Camera, Indonesian Troops Recover Body Of US Pilot Killed By Papua Rebels
content: Nicholas F Goselin, a pilot for Indonesian aviation company PT AMA, was shot dead Thursday shortly after he landed at the Ipdeheik airstrip in the mountainous province of Papua Highlands.

title: Crypto Horror In Gang-Rape Case Linked To Pakistan Deputy PM's Grandson
content: The allegations involve Raza Dar, who, the news agency PTI, citing police sources, said, is the grandson of Pakistan's Deputy Prime Minister and Foreign Minister Ishaq Dar.

title: Iran Confirms Hormuz Transit Fees, Offers "Special Treatment" For "Friends"
content: The strait normally carries one-fifth of the world's crude oil and liquefied natural gas, but it was all but closed by Iran during the Middle East war, sending energy prices soaring.

title: "He Knows Who The Boss Is": Trump On Possible Netanyahu White House Visit
content: The possible meeting comes amid reports of tensions between the two leaders over efforts to end the war against Iran  that the US and Israel started in late February.

title: "Case Against Adani Should Never Have Been Brought": US Justice Department
content: The United States pretending to be the world police can cause diplomatic strife and also wastes resources better spent on domestic concerns, said the DOJ.

title: Centre Lifts Curbs On Natural Gas Supplies As Middle East Tensions Ease
content: India in March invoked emergency measures to divert gas supplies from non-priority sectors to key users.

title: Amid Row, Devotees Asked to Remove IDs With Bhagwant Mann's Photo At Golden Temple
content: The Shiromani Gurdwara Parbandhak Committee (SGPC)  has convened a Panthic congregation over the controversial viral video involving the Punjab chief minister on Sunday.

title: "Kill Me To Stop Me": Defiant Mamata Banerjee Calls Trinamool Rebels "Traitors"
content: Mamata Banerjee said the Trinamool's symbol would remain with the bloc loyal to her, indicating that the rebels might face a protracted legal battle.

title: 'Courts Can't Second-Guess Prosecutors': US Department Of Justice On Adani Case
content: The department said courts have traditionally accepted brief motions seeking dismissal of criminal cases because demanding more detailed explanations could expose privileged internal deliberations.

title: "Not A Single Penny Lost": US Department Of Justice On Dropping Adani Charges
content: The Justice Department said this substantially weakened the criminal prosecution and weighed heavily in favour of dismissing the charges.

title: Southwest Monsoon To Cover Parts Of Gujarat, Punjab, Haryana In Next 4 days
content: The southwest monsoon is set to advance further into parts of the North Arabian Sea, Gujarat, the remaining areas of Haryana and Punjab, and more parts of Rajasthan over the next four days, the India Meteorological Department (IMD) said on Saturday.

title: 16-Year-Old Crushed Under Gravel While Unloading Dumper In Gurugram
content: A 16-year-old boy died after being buried under a load of gravel being unloaded from a dumper truck in Gurugram's Sector 113, officials said on Saturday, adding that the police have booked the accused driver.

title: Israel Doesn't Identify Who It Abused. 2 Gazan Mothers Claim It's Their Son
content: The photo depicts a blindfolded Gaza man stripped to his underwear and restrained face down on a cot while in Israeli custody.

title: Woman, 32, Raped By Neighbour In Jharkhand: Cops
content: A 32-year-old woman was allegedly raped by a neighbour in Jharkhand's Dumka district, police said on Saturday.

title: "Zeal Of Brimstone Preacher": JD Vance Salms US Critics In July 4th Address
content: Vance's speech came as dozens of tall ships from more than 20 countries sailed into New York harbour to celebrate the 250th anniversary of the signing of the Declaration of Independence on July 4, 1776.

title: 'Not Infighting': Punjab Congress Chief Plays Down Charanjit Channi Meeting
content: Reacting to the meeting at Channi's residence, Warring said Congress leaders and workers gathering there should not be viewed as a sign of factionalism.

title: In A First, Ladakh Deploys 100 Ex-Armymen To Curb Eco Violations, Off-Roading
content: The move comes days after tourists were fined for driving through ecologically sensitive areas near Pangong Lake, as the administration steps up enforcement to protect the Union Territory's fragile environment.

title: The empire of doubt, ally of convenience: America at 250, seen from India
content: Indrajeet Rai is the editor for TOI Online. He holds a PhD in International Politics from JNU. He has experience in both research and the social sector. He enjoys the fast-paced world of “breaking news”—as well as the equally important work of breaking down news for readers. Read More

title: 'Arey achcha toh chal raha hai': Suryakumar's response to India question goes viral
content: At TOI Sports Desk, reporters work around the clock to bring you comprehensive updates from the world of sports.
